title: secale cereale subsp. derzhavinii
intro: Secale cereale subsp. derzhavinii, also known as wild rye or Russian ryegrass, is a subspecies of the rye plant that belongs to the family Poaceae. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, including antifungal and anti-inflammatory compounds, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues. The health benefits of Secale cereale subsp. derzhavinii include its potential to help with digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and support immune function. The herbal preparations of wild rye often involve infusions or decoctions of the plant's roots and leaves, which can be used to create teas, tinctures, and other supplements.
